summary: Completed comprehensive testing of The Imperium app - Iteration 4. Tested major content upgrade: full recipes (30 with ingredients/method/cost/tip), expanded doctrines with Vel'nar forms, live chat translator, and Vel'nar Language Guide. Fixed critical bug: Dashboard was rendering recipe objects directly instead of recipe names. Backend: 26/28 tests passed (93%). Frontend: All new features working correctly.

minor.issue: Endpoint does not exist (404). Not a real issue - dashboard data comes from other endpoints.
Endpoint is /api/warrior/practices not /api/warrior-practices. Test file had wrong path.

integration_issues.issue: FIXED: Dashboard was rendering recipe objects directly instead of recipe.name. The /api/meals/plan/{day} endpoint now returns full recipe objects, but Dashboard.jsx was trying to render them as strings.

passed_tests: GET /api/meals/plan returns 30 recipes with full data (ingredients array, method array, cost, tip, serves)
GET /api/meals/plan/1 returns resolved recipes with full details for Day 1
GET /api/doctrines/expanded returns 6 doctrines each with: full_text, vel_nar_form, vel_nar_pronunciation, when_to_use, explanation
GET /api/velnar/language-guide returns root_houses (6), core_roots (12), heart_phrases (4 categories), shadow_lexicon (8), rite_phrases (5 categories), stage_path (5), script_system
Meal Plan page: renders Day 1-7 for Week 1, click recipe card expands to show ingredients/method/tip
Meal Plan page: Week navigation arrows work, All Recipes tab shows categorized recipes, Strategy tab shows batch strategy and budget tips
Translator page: is a chat interface with text input at bottom and message area above
Translator page: settings gear opens MBTI type selectors and direction toggle
POST /api/translator/translate returns translated text (AI-powered via Gemini)
Doctrines Panel: click opens panel from sidebar, click a doctrine shows full expanded view with Vel'nar form, pronunciation, and when-to-use
Vel'nar Language Guide page: renders root houses with color-coded cards, core roots list, all 8 tabs work (Roots, Sound, Formation, Phrases, Shadow Lexicon, Rite Phrases, Stages, Script)
All 15 navigation items render correctly in sidebar plus The Doctrines button
All existing pages still work (Dashboard, Tutor, DailyPlanner, WarriorPractices, Rite, Meditations, Calendar, ShadowWork, Training, Nutrition, Preparations, Glossary)
critical_code_review_comments: Dashboard.jsx was rendering recipe objects directly. When API response structure changed from string to object, the component needed to access recipe.name instead of the whole object. Fixed by checking typeof and extracting .name property.

success_rate.backend: 93% (26/28 tests passed - 2 minor endpoint naming issues)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (all new features working after Dashboard fix)

seed_data_creation: None - using existing data from meal_plan_full.py, expanded_doctrines.py, velnar_language.py
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 4 tested the major content upgrade. All new features working: 30 full recipes with ingredients/method/cost/tip, expanded doctrines with Vel'nar forms and line-by-line for Litany, live chat translator (Gemini AI), and comprehensive Vel'nar Language Guide with 8 tabs. Fixed Dashboard bug where recipe objects were being rendered directly. Backend endpoints: /api/meals/plan, /api/meals/plan/{day}, /api/meals/recipe/{key}, /api/doctrines/expanded, /api/doctrines/expanded/{key}, /api/velnar/language-guide, /api/translator/translate. The translator uses Gemini AI - may get 429 rate limit errors which are handled gracefully.

rca_of_the_issue.issue: React runtime error: Objects are not valid as a React child
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The /api/meals/plan/{day} endpoint was updated to return full recipe objects instead of recipe keys/names. Dashboard.jsx was directly rendering mealPlan.breakfast, mealPlan.lunch, mealPlan.dinner which were now objects instead of strings.
reproduction_steps: 2. App shows 'Uncaught runtime errors' with message about objects not being valid React children
3. Error trace shows the issue is with rendering recipe objects with keys {name, category, serves, prep_cook, cost, ingredients, method, tip}
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Modified Dashboard.jsx lines 150, 154, 158 to check if meal is an object and extract .name property: typeof mealPlan.breakfast === 'object' ? mealPlan.breakfast?.name : mealPlan.breakfast
rca_of_the_issue.prevention: When API response structures change, ensure all consuming components are updated to handle the new structure. Consider using TypeScript for better type safety.
